title: Asking Someone Out on a First Date

all.en: For weeks, I had been thinking about asking Anna from my book club out on a date.
all.zh: 好幾個星期以來，我一直在考慮邀請讀書會的安娜出去約會。

all.en: She was smart, kind, and always had interesting opinions about the novels we discussed.
all.zh: 她聰明、善良，對我們討論的小說總是有獨到的見解。

all.en: Every time I tried to bring it up, my courage disappeared at the last second.
all.zh: 每次我試圖開口時，我的勇氣總在最後一刻消失。

all.en: My friends teased me and said I needed to take a chance before someone else did.
all.zh: 我的朋友們取笑我，說我得趁別人之前先試試看。

all.en: After our next meeting, I decided to walk her to the bus stop and finally speak up.
all.zh: 在下一次聚會後，我決定陪她走到公車站，並終於開口。

all.en: My hands were sweating and my voice trembled as I asked if she would like to have dinner.
all.zh: 當我問她是否願意一起吃晚餐時，我的手心冒汗、聲音顫抖。

all.en: To my surprise, she smiled brightly and said she had been hoping I would ask.
all.zh: 令我驚訝的是，她燦爛地笑了，並說她一直希望我會開口。

all.en: We chose a small Italian restaurant on a quiet street near the river.
all.zh: 我們選了一間位於河邊安靜街道上的小型義大利餐廳。

all.en: During the dinner, we discovered that we loved the same movies and shared similar dreams.
all.zh: 晚餐期間，我們發現我們喜歡相同的電影，也擁有相似的夢想。

all.en: The conversation flowed so naturally that two hours passed without either of us noticing.
all.zh: 對話進行得如此自然，兩個小時不知不覺就過去了。

all.en: As we said goodnight, we both agreed there should definitely be a second date soon.
all.zh: 當我們道晚安時，我們都同意應該儘快有第二次的約會。
